title: Quiz: Free Energy Methods
description: Test your understanding of free energy calculations

questions.question: What does ΔG < 0 indicate about a process?
questions.explanation: A negative Gibbs free energy change (ΔG < 0) indicates a spontaneous process that will proceed without external input. The system naturally moves toward lower free energy.

questions.question: What is the key equation in thermodynamic integration?
questions.explanation: Thermodynamic integration calculates ΔG by integrating the ensemble average of ∂H/∂λ over the coupling parameter λ from 0 to 1, connecting initial and final states.

questions.question: What is the purpose of umbrella sampling?

options.text: To prevent water molecules from evaporating
To bias sampling along a reaction coordinate

questions.explanation: Umbrella sampling adds harmonic biasing potentials (umbrellas) to force sampling in regions that would otherwise be rarely visited, allowing construction of free energy profiles along reaction coordinates.

questions.question: What does 'alchemical' mean in free energy calculations?

options.text: Transforming one molecule into another through non-physical paths

questions.explanation: Alchemical transformations gradually change one molecule into another (e.g., ligand A to B) using a coupling parameter λ. Though the path is non-physical, the free energy difference is valid because G is a state function.

questions.question: The potential of mean force (PMF) is the free energy as a function of a reaction coordinate.
questions.explanation: True. The PMF, G(ξ), gives the free energy profile along a chosen reaction coordinate ξ. It's computed from the probability distribution: G(ξ) = -kT ln P(ξ) + constant.

questions.question: Which methods can compute binding free energies? (Select all)
questions.explanation: TI, FEP, and alchemical methods can compute binding free energies. Simple energy minimization gives only potential energy, not free energy, as it neglects entropy.
